intro: Codonopsis celebica, also known as Celebes Codonopsis or Celebes Balloon Flower, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Campanulaceae family, native to the Celebes Island in Indonesia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory properties, which may help to alleviate conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Codonopsis celebica include saponins, glycosides, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Codonopsis celebica is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as being incorporated into soups and tonics, where it is valued for its purported ability to tonify the spleen and boost energy.
